danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based hostile entities in level environment
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring avoidance or defeat; contact causes health loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental obstacles (implied from level design descriptions)
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles that damage player on contact; requires platforming skill to navigate

danger_signals.visual_form: Mr. Blizzard (yeti) as final antagonist; visually distinct from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring sustained engagement; represents narrative antagonist attempting world domination

safety_signals.meaning: Safe to collect; provides points and completion bonuses; indicates explored areas
Resource replenishment; safe recovery points within level space

safety_signals.visual_form: Level completion state; passwords in most versions
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression markers; ability to resume from checkpoint indicates security of progress

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled sprite; appears in all six themed levels pursuing goal of stopping Mr. Blizzard

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct boss sprite; visually larger/more threatening than standard enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with ice/frozen world state; final level confrontation; uses magic powers to turn world into ice

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Various themed enemy sprites per level
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different designs across six themed level environments
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout levels; defeatable with single strike (except bosses); indicate level theme hazards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six themed level environments with distinct visual characteristics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard enemy patterns; collectible resources available; world remains stable and navigable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Thematic level designs suggest regional environments; ice/frozen aesthetic implied as part of antagonist's goal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate through hostile environments; defeat enemies; reach Mr. Blizzard as final confrontation to prevent world takeover

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition from current level to next; password display in non-SNES versions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player achieves goal state; receives completion bonus based on coin collection; can continue to next themed level

environmental_storytelling.example: Six distinct themed level environments communicate regional variation and world composition; each level presents unique hazard types and enemy configurations appropriate to its theme

Mr. Nutz as red squirrel protagonist establishes character identity; Philippe Dessoly's pencil sketches and design choices communicate protagonist as capable, agile hero suitable for platforming challenges
Mr. Blizzard's characterization as yeti with world-domination goal through ice magic establishes high-stakes narrative; positioning as final boss communicates escalating threat
Hidden coins, health, and extra lives distributed throughout levels communicate world state as containing remnants of value to salvage; encourages thorough exploration

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can run, jump, swim in select levels, collect items, strike enemies with tail, throw collected nuts; demonstrates capability appropriate to hero archetype
Positioned in level environment; defeatable with single strike or nut projectile; cause health loss on contact; retreat when defeated
Requires sustained engagement (not single-strike defeat); demands multiple nut strikes or tail attacks; represents culminating challenge to player progression
Contact with hazards/inadequately defeated enemies causes health loss; brief invulnerability flashing follows damage; losing all health costs one life; restart from level beginning on life loss

notes: Mr. Nutz communicates world-state and danger primarily through environmental theming and entity positioning rather than explicit UI overlays. The narrative of stopping Mr. Blizzard's ice-based world domination provides thematic coherence to level design. Danger is signaled through enemy placement and hazard positioning within themed environments. Safety is communicated through collectible arrangement and level structure that rewards thorough exploration. The game lacks explicit faction warfare mechanics but establishes clear protagonist-antagonist relationship through character design and narrative framing. Visual communication relies on sprite-based character differentiation and level environmental aesthetics to convey world state and threat assessment.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Mr. Nutz SNES page)
